facts.label: AL-ABYAD, THE WHITES
facts.story: The UAE national team carries the nickname Al-Abyad, meaning The Whites, a direct nod to the all-white home shirt with red trim that has defined the side since its earliest years. The away kit flips the palette to red with white trim, a pairing so consistent that decades later, kit designers still return to it as the template. When the UAE walked out for their World Cup debut in 1990, they did so in the same white shirt supporters had known them by for years.

facts.label: THE FIRST-EVER GOAL
facts.story: Two minutes after halftime against West Germany at the Stadio San Nicola, with the score already 2-0, Khalid Ismail Mubarak turned a quick counter-attack into the goal that mattered most in UAE football history: the country's first ever at a World Cup. West Germany went on to win 5-1, but Mubarak's strike in the 46th minute is the moment UAE fans still point to from their only appearance on the sport's biggest stage.

annotations.label: THE FIRST-EVER GOAL
annotations.story: Trailing West Germany 2-0, Khalid Ismail Mubarak scored in the 46th minute on a quick counter-attack, the first goal the UAE had ever scored at a World Cup. West Germany finished the job, winning 5-1, but the goal remains a landmark for Emirati football.

annotations.label: A CONSOLATION AGAINST YUGOSLAVIA
annotations.story: In their final group match, already eliminated, the UAE fell behind early to Yugoslavia before Ali Thani Juma'a pulled one back in the 22nd minute. Yugoslavia pulled away to win 4-1, closing out a winless debut in which the UAE conceded 11 goals across three matches.
